objective: Supports innovators and startups in converting technology ideas into functional prototypes through funding, mentoring, infrastructure and technical guidance.
eligibility: Innovators and startups apply through approved NIDHI-PRAYAS Centres or Advance PRAYAS Centres, subject to the current programme guidelines.
benefits: Prototype funding, fabrication and laboratory access, technical and business mentoring, IP/legal assistance and industry or investor connections.
funding_summary: Current PRAYAS 2.0 information states support up to INR 20 lakh through PRAYAS Centres and up to INR 40 lakh through Advance PRAYAS Centres.
application_process: Apply to an approved PRAYAS Centre or Advance PRAYAS Centre when that centre announces an application window.
source_evidence: Official NIDHI pages explicitly identify innovators and startups as beneficiaries and explain the centre-mediated application route.

objective: Encourages graduate students and aspiring technology entrepreneurs to pursue entrepreneurial ventures by reducing early-stage personal and business risk.
eligibility: Aspiring entrepreneurs and innovators apply through approved NIDHI-EIR Centres, subject to the current centre call and guidelines.
benefits: Financial and non-financial fellowship support, mentoring and an incubation pathway for developing a technology venture.
funding_summary: Fellowship amount and tenure must be confirmed from the current official guideline or centre call.
application_process: Apply through an approved NIDHI-EIR Centre when applications are invited.
source_evidence: Official NIDHI EIR page states that students and innovators apply through selected NIDHI-EIR Centres.

objective: Provides seed support through approved incubators to promising startups developing innovative technologies, products and services.
eligibility: Potential and incubatee startups apply to listed supported incubators or TBIs under their periodic calls.
benefits: Seed support for product development, market entry and progression from technology development toward commercialisation.
funding_summary: Official programme information states support to a deserving startup may be up to INR 100 lakh, subject to incubator assessment and current guidelines.
application_process: Apply to a supported Startup Incubator or TBI when it announces a NIDHI-SSP call.
source_evidence: Official NIDHI-SSP page explicitly identifies startups as beneficiaries and requires application through supported incubators.

objective: Creates and supports technology business incubators that provide workspace, facilities, mentoring and venture-development assistance to innovators and startups.
eligibility: Innovators and startups apply to individual DST-supported TBIs under each incubator's admission criteria and periodic calls.
benefits: Incubation, common facilities, laboratories, co-working space, mentoring, networks and venture-development support.
funding_summary: Support to startups varies by incubator and linked NIDHI programme; verify the relevant TBI call.
application_process: Select a relevant DST-supported TBI and apply under its current incubatee admission process.
source_evidence: Official NIDHI-TBI page states that innovators and startups may apply to DST-supported TBIs, which periodically announce enrollment calls.

secondary_sectors: Inclusive Entrepreneurship; Tier-2 and Tier-3 Ecosystems
objective: Strengthens inclusive innovation and entrepreneurship ecosystems, especially in regions where startup support is at an early stage.
eligibility: Students, innovators, researchers and entrepreneurs access support through an operating NIDHI-iTBI and its intake process.
benefits: Incubation, mentoring, entrepreneurial capacity building and support for converting ideas into startups.
funding_summary: Startup-level support varies by iTBI and call; verify the selected iTBI's current opportunity.
application_process: Apply to a relevant operating NIDHI-iTBI when it invites innovators or startups.
source_evidence: Official NIDHI-iTBI page explicitly describes support for innovators, entrepreneurs and startup creation.

objective: Fast-tracks the growth of promising post-incubation startups through structured mentoring, market validation, networking and investor exposure.
eligibility: Startups participate through accelerator cohorts operated by eligible supported TBIs; individual cohort criteria apply.
benefits: Three-to-six-month structured acceleration, mentoring, networking, customer validation, investment readiness and demo-day exposure.
funding_summary: Programme funding is provided to eligible TBIs for accelerator cohorts; startup benefits are delivered through the cohort.
application_process: Apply to a NIDHI-supported accelerator cohort when an implementing TBI opens applications.
source_evidence: Official NIDHI Accelerator page describes a post-incubation programme for potential startups delivered through existing TBIs.

objective: Provides financial assistance for development and commercial application of indigenous technology or wider domestic application of adapted technology.
eligibility: Eligible Indian industrial concerns, including qualifying DPIIT-recognised startups incorporated as companies, may apply under TDB guidelines.
benefits: Financial assistance through loan, equity and, in exceptional cases, grant support for eligible technology-commercialisation projects.
funding_summary: Official TDB FAQ states qualifying DPIIT-recognised startups with projects costing up to INR 5 crore may be funded up to 70 percent of project cost, subject to appraisal and guidelines.
application_process: Submit a project proposal through TDB's official process using the current application form, checklist and funding guidelines.
source_evidence: Official TDB FAQ explicitly states startup eligibility and a startup-specific funding proportion.
